ground_truth:
Cardiac Catheterization Report
Hosp: City General Hospital  Date: 14/01/2024
Pt: David Brown  DOB: 22/07/1958  MRN: 772319

Indication: Progressive unstable angina, positive stress test.

Procedure: Left heart catheterization with coronary angiography
Access: Right femoral artery, 6Fr sheath

Hemodynamics:
  LV end-diastolic pressure: 12 mmHg
  Aortic pressure: 130/80 mmHg
  LVEDV: Normal
  LVEF: 55% (visually estimated)

Coronary Anatomy:
  LMCA: No significant disease
  LAD: 70% stenosis in mid-segment, TIMI 3 flow
  LCx: 40% stenosis in proximal segment
  RCA: Dominant, 85% stenosis in mid-segment, TIMI 3 flow

Valvular Assessment:
  Aortic: No gradient, trace regurgitation
  Mitral: Mild regurgitation, no stenosis

Impression: Two-vessel CAD (LAD + RCA).
Recommend PCI with drug-eluting stents.

Dr. A. Patel, MD, FSCAI
Interventional Cardiology
